pi_items.source: Install script (install_vitalrecorder.ps1) specifies download URL: https://vitaldb.net/getvr.php?type=msi&ver=1.16.6 with fallback to 1.16.4. Environment evidence document confirms version 1.16.6 installed.

File confirmed to exist at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/vital_recorder_windows_env/data/0003.vital (gzip compressed). Setup script copies this file to C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\VitalRecorderData\0003.vital for task execution.

pi_items.metadata_value: VitalDB open dataset from Seoul National University Hospital
pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB - open access dataset from Seoul National University Hospital (6,388 surgical cases, published in Nature Scientific Data)
pi_items.source: Evidence document (evidence_docs/README.md) confirms: 'Data Source: VitalDB Open Dataset (real surgical case recordings)'. Web verification via PMC article (https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9178032/) confirms VitalDB contains non-cardiac surgeries from Seoul National University Hospital collected Aug 2016 - Jun 2017.

pi_items.verified_value: Non-cardiac surgical case (all VitalDB cases are non-cardiac: general, thoracic, urological, or gynecological surgeries)
pi_items.source: VitalDB scientific publication (Nature Scientific Data, PMC9178032) explicitly states: 'Data were obtained from non-cardiac (general, thoracic, urological, and gynecological) surgery patients' at Seoul National University Hospital. Dataset breakdown: general 77%, thoracic 17%, urological 2%, gynecological 4%.

Evidence document (evidence_docs/README.md) Task 5 section states: 'Start state: Vital Recorder with 0003.vital loaded in Track mode, showing ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH, COMPLIANCE, INSP_SEVO, EXP_SEVO, PAMB_MBAR, MAWP_MBAR, PPLAT_MBAR tracks'

Actual file size verified: /data/0003.vital is 6,331,392 bytes (6.3 MB). When decompressed from gzip, expands to 24,975,256 bytes (23.82 MB). Evidence document cites 6.5MB which is approximately correct for the compressed file.
Evidence document (evidence_docs/README.md) Task 5 data table: '0003.vital ... 1h 13m 14s real surgical case'

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiple waveform traces from medical devices (ECG, arterial pressure, pulse oximetry, sevoflurane, compliance, airway pressure)
pi_items.verified_value: ECG_II, ECG_V5, PLETH, COMPLIANCE, INSP_SEVO, EXP_SEVO, PAMB_MBAR, MAWP_MBAR, PPLAT_MBAR
pi_items.source: Evidence document lists tracks for 0003.vital. Binary file analysis confirms header contains device names: SNUADC (multiparameter monitor), Solar 8000 (ventilator), Primus (ventilator), Orchestra (anesthesia workstation), BIS (bispectral monitor).

pi_items.verified_value: Event markers present: 'Case started', 'Surgery started', 'Surgery finished', 'Case finished'
pi_items.source: Binary file analysis of 0003.vital reveals EVENT section with string markers. Offsets found: 'Surgery started' at offset 339, indicating standard VitalRecorder event structure with all four expected surgical phase markers.
VitalDB scientific publication and PhysioNet repository documentation confirm dataset contains 6,388 intraoperative vital sign cases with 486,451 waveform and numeric data tracks.

privileged_info_summary: Case 0003 is a real noncardiac surgical case from the VitalDB open dataset maintained at Seoul National University Hospital. The 1-hour 13-minute recording contains high-fidelity vital sign monitoring data including electrocardiograms (ECG_II, ECG_V5), pulse oximetry (PLETH), respiratory mechanics (COMPLIANCE), volatile anesthetic agent concentrations (INSP_SEVO, EXP_SEVO), and airway pressures (PAMB_MBAR, MAWP_MBAR, PPLAT_MBAR). The data includes standard event markers for surgical phases (Surgery started, Surgery finished). Vital Recorder version 1.16.6 is used to display this data in Track mode showing waveform strips with timeline controls. The dataset is publicly available and commonly used for anesthesia research and machine learning applications.
